name: Support Life Foundation
mission: Our goal is to alleviate the various forms of oppression in society by developing unique programs that maximize impact, minimize cost, and bring everyone together in support.
theoryOfChange: Utilizing the power of volunteerism to create regenerative solutions that maximize impact and minimize cost to alleviate oppression.
scoreSummary: Support Life Foundation shows good impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.detail: Organization lists international operations but IRS filings show no foreign grant activity.

score_details.score_summary: Support Life Foundation shows good impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: No explicit mention of "zakat" or "zakat donations" was found on the official website of Support Life Foundation (www.supportlives.org) or its donation pages in the provided search results. While Support Life Foundation is involved in "Free Food Distribution" via the Muslim Community Association (MCA) of San Francisco Bay Area, and MCA lists a "Zakah Request" service, this does not constitute explicit acceptance of zakat donations by Support Life Foundation itself. (Source: https://www.mcabayarea.org/mec-events/free-food-distribution-sign-up-required-via-support-life-foundation/)

all_citations.claim: The organization holds a Charity Navigator score of 76.0/100 and maintains a 95.1% program expense ratio.
Support Life Foundation reported total revenue of $1,078,197 and holds 7.1 months of working capital.
Key programs include Project Educate, The Hiring, and You Are Not Alone: Community Food Share.
The organization offers various donation options for supporters to contribute sadaqah.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial efficiency and low overhead, balanced against a need for more robust external impact data.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing third-party impact verification to improve overall transparency scores
Expanding public documentation regarding long-term program outcomes

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is rated at 32/50, representing a medium fit for donors interested in humanitarian causes and social justice [3]. The organization is identified as sadaqah-eligible, offering a verified pathway for general charitable giving within the community [4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates a moderate impact score of 32/50, reflecting its role in capacity building through community food shares and educational programs [3]. While financial health is strong with a 76.76.0/100 from Charity Navigator score, the cost per beneficiary is calculated at 587.9435, indicating a resource-intensive model for direct service delivery [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Support Life Foundation is a community-based organization dedicated to social and economic justice through high-efficiency programming.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 95.1%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach beneficiaries
Strong fundraising efficiency with a cost of $0.00 per $1 raised
Healthy liquidity with 7.1 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: Based in New York, the Support Life Foundation provides essential services designed to minimize costs for underserved communities through initiatives like Project Educate and The Hiring [3]. The organization operates with a high degree of financial transparency, reporting total revenue of $1,078,197 and maintaining a program expense ratio of 95.1% [1][2].

confidence.notes: Strong financial transparency despite governance limitations.

alignment.explanation: The charity is a strong fit for sadaqah-seeking donors, serving <cite id="6">underserved populations</cite> and addressing <cite id="6">high-urgency humanitarian needs</cite>.
alignment.improvement: To improve alignment with Muslim donors, the organization could <cite id="6">add a dedicated page</cite> clarifying the religious context of its humanitarian services.

credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the foundation should <cite id="7">expand its board of directors</cite> beyond the current three members to enhance independent oversight.

impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="7">documenting a clear theory of change</cite> with an explicit causal pathway for its various international programs.

rich_narrative.headline: Support Life Foundation is a community-based organization dedicated to social and economic justice through high-efficiency programming.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize high financial efficiency and want their sadaqah to support a mix of domestic California-based aid and West African health initiatives.
donor_motivations: Value extremely low overhead and zero fundraising costs
Support community-based social and economic justice
Interested in supporting health and education in Sierra Leone and Cameroon
Prefer organizations with high transparency seals (Candid Platinum)
giving_considerations: The organization is smaller than global peers like CARE or Oxfam
Revenue has decreased recently, meaning new donations have high marginal utility
Governance is centralized within a small board
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors specifically seeking to fulfill Zakat obligations or those who require a large, institutionalized board of directors.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization tracks <cite id="7">outcome metrics</cite> and has a clear goal to <cite id="7">house 250 refugee families</cite>, but lacks independent RCT evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The foundation aims to reduce poverty and improve health by providing direct services (meals, education, healthcare) that minimize costs for the underserved.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that high program spending translates into measurable improvements in beneficiary well-being.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="6">negative revenue growth rate</cite> and ambitious goals like <cite id="7">distributing 5 million pounds of food</cite>, the foundation has significant capacity to absorb new capital.

peer_comparison.differentiator: The foundation's <cite id="1">95.1% program ratio</cite> exceeds the <cite id="6">peer median of 88.2%</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="1">95.1% of its total expenses toward programs</cite>, outperforming the <cite id="6">peer program ratio median of 88.2%</cite>.
The foundation maintains <cite id="1">7.1 months of working capital</cite>, providing a strong financial cushion for its humanitarian operations.
Support Life Foundation holds a <cite id="7">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of disclosure available.

strengths_deep_dive: The Support Life Foundation demonstrates a remarkable ability to scale its impact with minimal overhead. According to its <cite id="2">2023 Form 990 filing</cite>, the organization managed over $1.2 million in contributions with <cite id="1">zero fundraising expenses</cite>. This lean operational model allows for a diverse range of programs, from <cite id="3">Teacher For Every Village</cite> in West Africa to local <cite id="3">Hot Meals programs</cite> in California. Furthermore, the organization <cite id="8">meets all 20 BBB Wise Giving Alliance Standards</cite> for Charity Accountability, confirming its commitment to ethical governance and financial reporting.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2017, the Support Life Foundation is a community-based organization focused on social and economic justice through high-efficiency programming in the United States and West Africa. The organization operates with a lean financial model, reporting <cite id="1">total revenue of $1,228,678</cite> in 2023. Their mission is centered on providing programs that minimize costs while maximizing direct aid to underserved populations, specifically through initiatives like <cite id="3">Project Educate, Hot Meals, and the Health Center Yaoundé</cite>. The foundation maintains a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 95.1%</cite>, which is significantly higher than the <cite id="1">industry standard of 75%</cite>. This efficiency is further evidenced by a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating that nearly every dollar donated is directed toward service delivery rather than administrative overhead. Geographically, the foundation serves multiple counties in California, including <cite id="3">San Mateo, Alameda, and Sacramento</cite>, while also maintaining international operations in <cite id="3">Sierra Leone and Cameroon</cite>. Despite its high financial efficiency, the organization holds an <cite id="1">overall score of 76.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting a need for strengthened governance and accountability documentation.
